channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: What Happened to Trump the China Hawk?
item.description: The U.S. president heads to Beijing in detente and dealmaking mode.

item.title: America and China are shielding the world from an oil catastrophe
item.description: Will they lose their nerve before Hormuz reopens?

item.title: How Long Can Keir Starmer Hang On?
item.description: The British prime minister is barely clinging to power over a split and unpopular Labour Party.

item.title: What Trump and Xi Want From Their Summit
item.description: The leaders are expected to talk trade, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

item.title: China’s Rare-Earth Card Looms Over Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: For all of the U.S. leader’s efforts, Washington remains deeply vulnerable.

item.title: Chinese Hegemony Might Be Happening
item.description: There were always reasons to doubt it was a real possibility—until now.

item.title: What’s Driving the K-Shaped Economy?
item.description: Uneven wealth distribution in the United States has political—and global—implications.

item.title: Asia Isn’t as Peaceful as It Seems
item.description: Trouble is brewing as norms against war dissolve.

item.title: Index rebalancing is now the biggest event in markets
item.description: But profiting from it is another matter

item.title: The EU and China are stumbling into a trade war
item.description: Swamped by Chinese exports, Europe nears a point of no return

item.title: Asylum in America is all but over. It may never come back
item.description: How Donald Trump fulfilled a campaign pledge

item.title: Would We Be Better Off Today With the JCPOA?
item.description: The Iran war spotlights one of Washington’s most contentious counterfactuals.

item.title: Why many women cannot make enough breast milk
item.description: The causes are often beyond their control

item.title: Bashar al-Assad’s henchmen start to go on trial in Syria
item.description: But some worry that the process is too rushed to be credible

item.title: Why the U.S. Is Headed for a Long War With Iran
item.description: The Tehran regime is more hard-line than ever—and has nothing left to lose.

item.title: International Climate Law Needs Teeth
item.description: An advisory opinion from the International Court of Justice last year left no doubt that states have a legal obligation to prevent significant harm to the climate system, and that a failure to do so carries legal consequences. Now, a new United Nations resolution seeks to put this ruling into practice.

item.title: A 7-Year-Old American Boy Is Trapped in China Because His Father Made Art
item.description: In what can only be described as collective punishment, the young son of detained artist Gao Zhen is being prevented from returning home to the United States

item.title: The Philippines impeaches its vice-president
item.description: Sara Duterte’s political career hangs in the balance

item.title: 4 Kazakh Regions Likely to See Water Shortages in 2026
item.description: Following the 2025 regional drought, Kazakhstan authorities have warned about below-average reservoir levels and the possibility for summer shortages.

item.title: Human Rights Are Off the Agenda at the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing
item.description: The meeting will be a stark display of Trump and Xi’s shared anti-human rights vision of transactional geopolitics. Accountability will have to come from elsewhere.

item.title: How I Became a Manufacturing Skeptic
item.description: Manufacturing used to be a powerful economic escalator because it could employ large numbers of low-skilled workers while making limited demands on low-income countries’ governance and infrastructure. The good news is that services may be able to deliver the productivity growth necessary to support a robust middle class.

item.title: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Police: Pakistan’s Underfunded Frontline
item.description: KP police are the most targeted by terrorists. They are also the worst paid.

item.title: The Coming Food Crisis in South Asia
item.description: Oil disruptions are cascading into fertilizer markets, weakening agriculture and putting South Asia’s food security at risk. We've seen this pattern before.

item.title: Don’t Fall for Rumors of Putin’s Weakness
item.description: Claims of cracks in the regime overlook the Russian leader’s mastery of dictatorship.

item.title: How China Became the First Airtight Empire
item.description: Beijing rewrote two slogans before it sealed the country – once to rewrite history, once to prewrite the future.

item.title: Turmoil at the Fed Won’t End Anytime Soon
item.description: It may be tempting to regard US President Donald Trump’s attacks on the Federal Reserve as a personal vendetta against outgoing Chair Jerome Powell. But Trump’s hostility reflects a broader trend: the erosion of conservative support for independent central banks worldwide.

item.title: India’s ‘China Reset’ Has No Answer to AVIC’s Pakistan Admission
item.description: New Delhi has hedged more toward China without articulating any conditions. Beijing is taking full advantage.

item.title: Rubio Aims to Reset U.S.-India Ties
item.description: The secretary of state may face an uphill battle during his visit to New Delhi this month.

item.title: Will Trump Betray Taiwan?
item.description: When Donald Trump sits down with Xi Jinping this week, he will be facing a leader who is patient, perceptive, and ambitious. While the best way to safeguard Taiwan’s autonomy is to ensure Xi never receives the signal that patience is no longer required, Trump's fecklessness could have the opposite effect.

item.title: Indonesia’s Fiscal Outlook in a Period of Divergent Signals
item.description: How to make sense of a number of apparently contradictory outlooks on the country's economic and fiscal health.

item.title: The Fuel Crisis Is Testing ASEAN’s Limits
item.description: A coordinated response from the grouping did not emerge from its Cebu summit.

item.title: 2 Kilogram of Ruby Unearthed in Myanmar’s Mogok Mining Town
item.description: The country's military government has claimed ownership of a rock worth millions of dollars.

item.title: What Pakistan Army Chief’s Speech Reveals About the Future of Conflict With India
item.description: Pakistan is more confident, both in its military capabilities and diplomatic space regionally and beyond, and has moved beyond a purely defensive posture

item.title: Ethnic Groups Are Fleeing Bangladesh’s Chittagong Hill Tracts in Search of Safety in Myanmar
item.description: They have been terrorized by the Bangladesh army, Muslim settlers from the plains, and armed ethnic outfits for decades.

item.title: The Response to Political Violence in 1968 Was Very Different From Today
item.description: Back then, political will existed to meet tragedy with law.

item.title: Can Trump Get a New Nuclear Deal With Iran?
item.description: Washington has more demands—and Tehran has more leverage.

item.title: How America Can Coerce the Cartels
item.description: A smarter war on drugs begins with selective punishment.

item.title: China Is Squandering a Golden Opportunity
item.description: Why Beijing has failed to exploit Trump’s missteps,

item.title: Philippine Senator Flees Arrest as ICC Unseals Warrant For ‘Drug War’ Killings
item.description: Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa has locked himself inside his Senate office in order to avoid arrest and extradition to The Hague-based court.

item.title: Philippine Lawmakers Vote to Impeach Vice President Sara Duterte For Second Time
item.description: The 47-year-old is accused of a range of crimes, including corruption, misuse of government funds, and plotting to kill her former ally, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.

item.title: The Hormuz Crisis and China’s Energy Security Dilemma
item.description: While Beijing has sought to diversify suppliers, expand storage capacity, and build alternative transport corridors, its energy security ultimately remains dependent on a few maritime chokepoints.
